source.title: Context-dependent memory in two natural environments: on land and underwater

confidence_rationale: The canonical empirical demonstration that ENVIRONMENTAL context at encoding gates later recall (the concrete instance of MECH-338's cue<->context-tag matching), but a high-powered 2021 pre-registered replication (Royal Society Open Science) failed to reproduce the effect. The principle MECH-338 rests on is independently secured by Tulving & Thomson; this entry is included for the concrete environmental instantiation but its confidence is deliberately low to register the robustness problem honestly.
failure_signatures: Original result: free recall was substantially better when the test environment matched the learning environment (land/underwater), directly instantiating context-as-retrieval-cue -- a retrieval gate insensitive to reinstated context would not show this.

ROBUSTNESS CONTRA: a 2021 pre-registered, higher-powered replication (Royal Society Open Science 8:200724) did NOT find a same-context advantage. The specific environmental-context-dependent-recall effect is fragile; MECH-338 must therefore rest on the general encoding-specificity principle (Tulving & Thomson) and on the neural substrate (Nakazawa CA3 pattern completion), NOT on the strength of the environmental-context phenomenon alone.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Divers learned word lists on land or underwater and recalled them 4 minutes later in the same or the other environment (all four combinations). Recall was markedly better when the recall environment matched the learning environment -- environmental context at encoding functions as a retrieval cue.
mapping.ree_translation: The concrete environmental instantiation of MECH-338's 'select' face: a candidate populated (encoded) in context C is more accessible when context C is reinstated at retrieval -- i.e. an environmental cue matched against a context tag gates availability. It demonstrates that the cue need not be the rule's content; a co-encoded context feature suffices, which is exactly what 'context-bound' means in MECH-337/338.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Two layers of caveat. (1) Domain: adult human verbal free recall, not candidate-rule retrieval -- analogical transfer. (2) ROBUSTNESS: the specific environmental effect did not survive a powered pre-registered replication (2021). This entry is retained for the concrete instantiation and to log the contra-evidence transparently, NOT as primary support; MECH-338's grounding leans on Tulving & Thomson (principle) and Nakazawa 2002 (substrate). Confidence is set low (0.58) to encode this.
mapping.source_context: Field experiment with trained divers; auditory word-list learning and free recall on land vs. ~6m underwater.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality marked DOWN (0.55) specifically because of the failed 2021 high-powered pre-registered replication -- the canonical status is historical, the effect is not robust. Mapping fidelity to MECH-338's environmental-cue case is good. Transfer risk moderate (verbal recall -> rule retrieval). Net confidence 0.58: included for the concrete instantiation, weighted down to register robustness honestly.
